entry.title: Unlocking Digital Excellence: Navigating SAP S/4HANA Transformation Strategically
entry.content:
So why did I use the 2003 legacy mobile phone the way I did? Because the limitations of the old technology did not allow me to work any different with it. The same is true with enterprises using the legacy technology. How they work with legacy ERP is determined by the guardrails and limitations of its 20th-century technology.

But approaching S/4 HANA (the 2024 smartphone example in my analogy) by migrating all the stuff from ECC into it means bringing over its limitations into a world where these limitations do not exist anymore. So we should approach SAP S/4 HANA the same way we approach smartphones in our private lives: Not backwards-looking but aligning the new capabilities with the challenges and needs of our situations and entirely using the aspects we will benefit most. By doing so, we will fully leverage the benefits of today's new technology capabilities.

For example, we are now experiencing a gradual shift to new sources of automotive fuel. Ten years ago, almost all cars ran on diesel and gasoline. Now, we see a electric vehicles on the road, but gas-powered cars will be nearly extinct in ten years. This will cause profound disruption that affects countless businesses. As the threat of climate change identified 50 years ago finally enters the public consciousness, the gradual shift will become sudden.

With today's ERP, you can't even talk seriously about innovation. It's like using a small shovel to mine for gold when you could be using an excavator. The excavator is SAP S/4HANA, the new ERP system with built-in intelligent technologies that are the foundation of a future-ready business.

Case-study and Business Challenge - How can the shipping Industry keep Pace with the Advancing Digital Transformation

With less than 40% of the commercial shipping fleet using even basic software services, you might think it is too premature to discuss digital transformation. However, change usually happens when no one is expecting it. Sea freight forwarding has stayed the same for too long as compared to land and air logistics and this is an opportune time for shipping to undergo a transformation to match customer expectations.

The old-fashioned business model of the shipping industry was centered on selling capacity alone, relying on time-consuming and sluggish processes like the handling of voluminous documentation and approvals.

Despite being an important cog in the wheel of supply chain solutions across the globe, shipping is yet to adopt the kind of digitization seen in the land-based logistics sector. One of the primary reasons for this was the connectivity issue and the highly fragmented nature of the segment.

Modernization and automation of port operations are among the factors that can drive port efficiency by effectively disseminating information and reducing paper-based manual workflows.

Building an SAP S/4HANA-based global business platform aligned with strategic goals

· More than 80% of all commercial cargo is transported via sea freight forwarding services; the digitization and visibility compared to other modes of transportation are very low. All this is set to change as technology enhancement allows better connectivity and taps opportunities that were missing earlier.

· Working with this knowledge, we began redesigning the Port Operation standard business platform based on SAP S/4 HANA. This design focused on business processes, IT architecture, and master data management, and it leveraged SAP CPI methodology, which introduced a range of standard business processes.

entry.title: How to delete amended record of timesheet for particular week into SuccessFactors?
entry.content:
I will not show how employee submit the timesheet and how approval process happens.

I will show the procedure directly, If employee tries to amend the approved timesheet then the original timesheet will be overridden and new timesheet record will be created.

In backend admin can only access these records using below navigation:

Manage data-> Employee timesheet

Example:

Employee has submitted the timesheet and it was approved.

First time System will generate one external code for a timesheet, if it’s amended by employee then it’s replaced with new external code

 

For example:

Employee had amended the approved timesheet and submitted for approval again:

Now the timesheet new external code generated by system and old record will be stored in replaces External code shown in below image.

In the backend the new External code will show as approved:

Replaced external code is old one and it will show as cancelled into backend, this replaced external code should be deleted from the system to avoid issues.

Replaces- 3fe750fcd3de4680a9c87dab52477b1e

To delete the replaces external code click on "permanently Delete entry" option.

Click on Yes to continue

After deletion of replaced external code system will show only one record/external code into trace for the particular week timesheet:

Note: 

If you want to do bulk deletion they you need to download the "employee timesheet" data via "Import and Export data" tool

then filter the cancelled external code and upload with "delete" operation.

Conclusion:

This type of issue comes when the employees are allowed to amend the timesheet after approval.

We should delete the replaced one and new one will reside into system with actual hours.

The deletion will avoid issue of multiple records in backend and we can keep only actual records for particular week.

entry.title: Recurring deduction future timeslice records mass deletion
entry.content:
Requirement:

Delete the future time slice data or pay components from recurring deductions portlet, we can use the below import process so that the a particular time slice record or a particular pay component will be removed from employees recurring deductions data.

Below sample employee has multiple records in recurring deductions, we must remove all pay component deductions which start from Jan.2026 for all employees.

Possible Solution:

Mass deletion of the required time slice and removing all deductions for employees can be done with the import.

Export data of existing employees recurring deductions with “Import and export data” with Generic object as “Recurring deduction” with including dependencies “Yes”.

  • To remove the deduction, pay components from future dated records –
  1. Open the file “Recurring Deduction-Recurring Items” to remove the Jan.2026 pay component records for the appropriate employees.
  2. Insert “DELIMIT” operation for the deductions which are not required as shown in below picture.
  3. Import the CSV file for generic object “Recurring Deduction-Recurring Items”.

 

  • To remove the Jan.2026 time slice for all the deductions available for user-

Open the file “Recurring Deduction.csv” this file will have all the employee recurring deduction records with employee number and the effective date of the deduction.

  1. Open the file “Recurring Deduction.csv” to remove the Jan.2026 time slice.
  2. Remove the timeslice records not required( as highlighted in below picture) or Insert “DELETE” operation for the deductions which are not required
  3. Import the CSV file for generic object “Recurring Deduction”.

Below screenshot shows the export file of recurringdeduction.csv

Below picture shows the import file which has the user records after removing the not required time slice.

For the below sample employee, Jan.2026 time slice is now removed from the employee recurring deductions portlet after the successful import.

This is how to delete Recurring Deductions from employee profile. Hope this blog is helpful.

entry.title: Re: SQL Anywhere on ARM (Apple Silicon)
entry.content:
Hi Chris

Some 4 years later, are there any news regarding a macOS arm64 based SQL Anywhere version?
As the tools we use are now arm64 based it's getting more and more complicated with an Intel based SQL Anywhere. For example using Eclipse or Oracle Data Modeller and trying to source the sa_config.sh file leads to an error because of missing arm64 based library files. So we have to use older Intel versions which is not possible for all apps.

Best regards,
Robert

entry.title: SAP Estende Suporte para Versões Antigas Até 2033
entry.content:
A Nova Estratégia da SAP para a Business Suite 7

A SAP decidiu que vai encerrar o suporte para a antiga Business Suite 7 e está pressionando seus clientes a atualizarem seus sistemas. No entanto, agora a empresa oferece uma alternativa para estender o uso desses sistemas – uma medida que beneficia tanto os clientes quanto a própria SAP.

Empresas que aderirem ao programa Rise with SAP poderão continuar utilizando seus sistemas antigos até o final de 2033. Originalmente, o suporte seria encerrado em 2027, e a manutenção estendida, que exige um custo adicional, seria encerrada em 2030. Isso significa que os clientes têm mais tempo para se adaptar, já que a migração para uma nova solução pode levar anos.

A SAP confirmou os planos e destacou que essa opção visa facilitar a transição para o Rise with SAP, garantindo a continuidade dos negócios de seus clientes. Essa nova alternativa é chamada de SAP ERP, private edition, transition option, mas mais detalhes, como os custos, só serão divulgados ao longo do primeiro semestre deste ano.

Apesar da prorrogação, a SAP deixou claro que o suporte para a Business Suite 7 terminará definitivamente em 2027. Para quem ainda não começou a transição, essa prorrogação pode ser um alívio, já que mais de 60% dos clientes ainda utilizam o ECC.

Em resumo: pague um pouco mais, respire até 2033 e tenha tempo para planejar a migração para a nova geração de produtos SAP.

Fonte:

https://www.handelsblatt.com/technik/it-internet/s-4hana-das-ist-saps-neue-strategie-zur-business-suite-7/100101152.html?paymentupdate=undefined
